notes: Thirty-second validated clue batch compiled from official school websites.
Clues intentionally avoid revealing enrollment size, grade span, or directional/proximity information because those are gameplay feedback channels.

clues.text: Official history says the school originally opened in 1998 as New Century Charter High School.
Its vision emphasizes inclusiveness, mutual respect, personal attention, environmental stewardship, and the arts.
The school describes its faculty-designed model as combining differentiated instruction, outdoor and place-based education, and an inclusive community culture.
Official materials highlight Hawbridge Outdoors, with excursions such as hiking, biking, camping, canoeing, horseback riding, and rock climbing.
Board pages list Kemp Watson-Ormond as chair.
Administrator pages identify Taneeka Hanna as Lower School Director and Alexis Crumel as Upper School Director.
Its mission says the school inspires academic excellence while nurturing curiosity, creativity, and imagination in an environment rich with warmth, kindness, and respect.
Official materials say the program rests on four pillars, including passion for excellence, universal values, a global perspective, and service to others.
The school publishes three basic tenets: respect all living things, respect all non-living things, and use grace and courtesy.
Campus materials describe over 36 acres of trails, woods, bridges, fields, and outdoor classroom spaces.
Board of Trustees pages identify Joseph Knox as chairperson.
Official materials say visitors will not see rows of desks or teachers lecturing in front of the class, but children working at tables, on mats, and in small groups.
Its mission says the school empowers students to be lifelong learners, innovative thinkers, and good citizens able to compete globally.
Official materials repeatedly describe the school as a safe, small school environment where every child feels seen, valued, and supported.
The school calls its community the Wolfpack.
Official pages say the campus operates on a year-round calendar.
The school says it focuses on developing the whole child through leadership, character, and personal responsibility.
Board materials say its regular board meetings are held on the third Tuesday of each month at 6 p.m.
Its mission says the school develops artists who inspire others through the integration of academic excellence, virtuous character, and disciplined artistry.
Official materials identify Hayes the Chameleon as the school mascot.
Its vision is organized around four action words: Inquire, Imagine, Innovate, and Inspire.
The school says its teaching philosophy combines artful thinking, learning by doing, and excellence in teaching.
Administrative pages list Gerard Falls as superintendent.
Contact materials say the school resides in the historic site of The Capitol Department Store in downtown Fayetteville.
Its mission says the school fulfills the vision of The Basic School by Dr. Ernest L. Boyer.
Official materials say the school places a huge emphasis on hands-on learning, best instructional practices, and arts integration.
The school explains The Basic School philosophy through four principles known as the 4 C's.
Official materials say learning is promoted through field trips, the arts, community partnerships, and other engaging experiences.
The school brands itself around the HIVE.
Board materials say meetings alternate between on-site meetings and conference calls, with the public welcome to attend.
